prompt_text: Best customer support platforms for manage all customer conversations in one unified inbox across chat email and social
response:
The strongest platforms for managing **all customer conversations in one unified inbox** across chat, email, and social are **Front, Zendesk, Help Scout, HubSpot Service Hub, Bird, and Crisp**; for many teams, **Front or Zendesk** are the most capable all‑rounders for true omnichannel support.[2][3][4][5][6]

Below is a concise breakdown to help you choose, focused specifically on *one inbox across channels*:

| Platform | Best for | Unified inbox coverage (chat, email, social, others) | Key advantages | Potential drawbacks |
|---------|----------|-------------------------------------------------------|----------------|---------------------|
| **Front** | Fast‑moving teams, B2B, shared inbox workflows | Email, shared inboxes, messaging; supports SMS, social & integrations in one workspace[3] | Feels like email but with help‑desk features; strong collaboration (assign, comment), SLAs, automation, AI assistance; great for multi‑inbox teams[3] | Less “traditional” ticketing; may be overkill for very small teams |
| **Zendesk** | Companies wanting full help desk + omnichannel | Unified dashboard for email, live chat, social, and more[3][4][5] | Mature ecosystem; tickets, knowledge base, routing, analytics; strong omnichannel support and integrations[3][4][5] | Can be complex to set up; costs add up as you scale and add channels |
| **Help Scout** | SMBs that live in email but want help‑desk powers | Shared email inbox, live chat, in‑app messaging; social via integrations[2][5] | Very intuitive; great shared inbox for email support; built‑in docs; collaborative workflows[2] | Social and some channels rely on third‑party integrations vs. deep native omnichannel |
| **HubSpot Service Hub** | Teams already on HubSpot CRM | Email, live chat, Facebook Messenger & other channels via a unified inbox[6] | Tight CRM + marketing + support in one; good for seeing full customer lifecycle; simple unified inbox for smaller teams[6] | Social and advanced help‑desk features not as deep as Zendesk/Front; some features gated on higher tiers |
| **Bird (formerly MessageBird)** | Omnichannel messaging across many global channels | Email, chat, voice, SMS, WhatsApp, and social in one inbox[1] | Very strong on messaging channels (SMS, WhatsApp, voice) plus email and social; AI to automate and enhance support[1] | Less “classic help desk”; best if you are heavily messaging‑first |
| **Crisp** | Startups/SMBs wanting live chat + shared inbox | Shared inbox for email, chat; supports social channels and messaging with AI features[2] | Affordable; modern UI; AI features; unified view of conversations across channels for smaller teams[2] | Not as enterprise‑grade as Zendesk/Front; fewer advanced routing/ITSM features |

### How to pick based on your situation

- **If you want an email‑like inbox that unifies all channels and is great for collaboration:**  
  Choose **Front**.[3] It centralizes individual and shared inboxes plus messaging, so you do not switch between Gmail, your help desk, and Slack, while AI assists with triage and automation.[3]

- **If you need a full, classic help desk with strong omnichannel and knowledge base:**  
  Choose **Zendesk**. It offers a **unified dashboard** for all communication channels (email, chat, social, etc.), ticket routing, and knowledge management tools.[3][4][5]

- **If your support is mostly email + chat and you want simplicity:**  
  Choose **Help Scout** or **Crisp**. Help Scout is built for teams managing email and support conversations collaboratively in a shared inbox.[2] Crisp provides a shared inbox with AI, supporting email, chat, and social for smaller teams.[2]

- **If you’re already using HubSpot for CRM/marketing:**  
  Choose **HubSpot Service Hub** so your unified inbox and tickets sit directly on top of your CRM, giving you a combined view of marketing, sales, and support.[6]

- **If you are messaging‑first (WhatsApp, SMS, voice) and need many channels globally:**  
  Choose **Bird**, whose unified inbox combines email, chat, voice, SMS, WhatsApp, and social media, plus AI automation.[1]

If you share your team size, budget range, and which channels (e.g., WhatsApp vs. Instagram vs. in‑app chat) are most important, I can narrow this to 2–3 concrete recommendations and a suggested stack.
